attractions.name: Baker-Berry Library
attractions.description: Baker-Berry Library is the main library complex at Dartmouth College, serving as a central hub for research, learning, and inspiration. It combines the historic 1928 Baker Memorial Library with the modern Berry Library addition, offering extensive collections, diverse study spaces, and unique academic resources. (Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.address: 25 North Main Street, Hanover, NH 03755 (Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.hours: Baker & Berry: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Sat-Sun 10:00 AM - 12:00 AM; 1902 Room: Open 24 Hours (Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service, Dartmouth Libraries)
highlights: 'The Epic of American Civilization murals by José Clemente Orozco, a National Historic Landmark ([National Park Service)', 'The 1902 Room, a 24-hour quiet study space for students (Dartmouth Libraries)', 'Jones Media Center, providing digital art, audio, and video production tools (Dartmouth Libraries)', 'Book Arts Workshop for letterpress printing and bookbinding classes (Dartmouth Libraries)', "Tower Room, a classic 'Harry Potter-style' reading room with views of the Green (Dartmouth Admissions)", "Baker Bell Tower, which offers bird's-eye views of campus during scheduled tours (Dartmouth Libraries)"]
attractions.tips: Visitors can access the Orozco murals in the lower-level Reserve Reading Room; self-guided audio tours and brochures are available. For a quiet experience, visit during the early morning or late evening, as the library is a primary student study hub. (Tripadvisor, National Park Service)
attractions.nearby: Dartmouth Green, Hood Museum of Art, Hopkins Center for the Arts, Rauner Special Collections Library (Dartmouth Libraries, National Park Service)

attractions.name: Buddy Teevens Stadium at Memorial Field
attractions.description: Buddy Teevens Stadium at Memorial Field is a historic 11,000-seat football stadium and track facility at Dartmouth College. Originally built in 1893 and completed in 1923, it serves as a memorial to Dartmouth students and alumni who served in various wars, primarily World War I. The facility features a state-of-the-art FieldTurf surface and the Dusty Rhodes Track. Dartmouth Sports, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: General Use: Open when no varsity practices or games are in progress. Ticket Office: Monday-Friday 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Event Box Office: Opens 1 hour before game time. Dartmouth Sports Guest Guide
attractions.days_open: Open daily for general use (subject to varsity schedules); Ticket office: Monday-Friday. Dartmouth Sports
attractions.admission: Single game tickets: Premium Chairback $30, Regular Chairback $25, Premium Bench $20, Regular Bench $15 (Adult); Youth prices range $10-$30. Free for general use when no varsity events are in progress. Dartmouth Sports Tickets
highlights: 'Home to Dartmouth Big Green football and outdoor track and field teams. [Dartmouth Sports', 'Features the Dusty Rhodes Track, a state-of-the-art eight-lane 400-meter urethane surface. Dartmouth Sports', 'Historical brick-and-concrete structure dedicated as a memorial to fallen soldiers. Stadium Journey', 'Equipped with an HD scoreboard and stadium lights for night events. Dartmouth Sports', 'Smallest football venue in the Ivy League, providing intimate views of the action. Stadium Journey']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance to avoid long lines and higher game-day prices. Use the free shuttle service from the Dewey Lot for football games. Be prepared for the elements as there is no overhang in the grandstands. Dartmouth Sports Guest Guide, Stadium Journey

attractions.name: Dartmouth Cemetery (Hanover Cemetery)
attractions.description: Established in 1771 by Dartmouth College founder Eleazar Wheelock, this historic 12-acre cemetery serves as the final resting place for eight college presidents and numerous notable local residents. It is a scenic and hilly wooded area located adjacent to the Dartmouth campus, now maintained as a town cemetery.
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (Daily) (Hanover NH)
attractions.admission: Free (Hanover NH)
highlights: 'Burial site of 8 Dartmouth College presidents, including founder Eleazar Wheelock ([Dartmouth)', 'Contains the graves of several notable 19th-century Black residents, such as Jane Wentworth (Spare Rib)', 'Features a significant population of old-growth white pines and native wildflower plantings (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine)', 'Includes approximately 1,778 historic gravestones and markers spread across 375 plots (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine)', 'Accessible via the historic Cemetery Lane behind the Class of 1953 Commons (Dartmouth)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should park in designated campus or town areas and avoid parking on the grass. The terrain is hilly and includes steep, sometimes overgrown steps leading to wooded ravines. Proper cemetery etiquette is required, including respecting the privacy of mourners and not disturbing the historic markers.
attractions.nearby: Dartmouth College Green, Hood Museum of Art, Baker-Berry Library, Mink Brook Nature Preserve, Nathan's Garden (Hanover NH, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Dartmouth Hall
attractions.description: Dartmouth Hall is an iconic academic building at Dartmouth College, originally constructed in 1784 and meticulously rebuilt in 1904 following a fire. Recently renovated in 2022, it serves as the home for several language departments and the Leslie Center for the Humanities, representing the historical heart of the campus. Wikipedia, Dartmouth News
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM (Typical administrative/academic hours); closed Saturday and Sunday Department of Spanish and Portuguese
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Standard academic hours) Department of Spanish and Portuguese
attractions.admission: Free (Open for public academic and event viewing) Department of Spanish and Portuguese
highlights: 'Historic 1784 foundation and 1904 brick reconstruction [Wikipedia', 'Home to the Department of Spanish and Portuguese and German Studies Dartmouth Scholars Programs', 'Features the recently renovated Lecture Room 105 for performances Wikipedia', 'Iconic white facade overlooking the Dartmouth Green Campus of Dartmouth College', 'Houses the Leslie Center for the Humanities Wikipedia']
attractions.tips: Visitors should explore the interior to see the modern renovations combined with historic architecture. It is best to visit during the academic year to experience the campus atmosphere, but be mindful of ongoing classes. Dartmouth Admissions Blog
attractions.nearby: The Dartmouth Green, Baker-Berry Library, Hood Museum of Art, Hopkins Center for the Arts Dartmouth Campus Map

attractions.name: Downtown Hanover (Main Street District)
attractions.description: Downtown Hanover is a quintessential New England district where the Appalachian Trail meets the Ivy League, centered around the picturesque Dartmouth Green. The area features a vibrant mix of over 40 boutique shops, international dining, and cultural institutions like the Hood Museum of Art and the Hopkins Center for the Arts. It serves as a bustling hub for both Dartmouth College students and the local community, maintaining a historic yet modern atmosphere (Explore Hanover, Visiting New England).
attractions.hours: Street access 24/7; Typical business hours: Mon-Sat 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (some businesses extend to 9:00 PM or later); Metered parking: Mon-Sat 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Town of Hanover)
attractions.admission: Free (Public district); individual business prices vary; Hopkins Center and Hood Museum may have specific fees or free days (Town of Hanover, Explore Hanover)
highlights: "The Appalachian Trail runs directly along Main Street, connecting hikers to the town's core ([Saving Places)", 'Home to the historic Dartmouth Green, a five-acre common and focal point of campus life (Visiting New England)', 'Houses the world-class Hood Museum of Art and the Hopkins Center for the Arts (Explore Hanover)', 'Features the Nugget Theaters, a historic independent cinema serving the community since 1916 (Yelp)', 'Host to vibrant community events like the Snack Town Street Fair and seasonal festivals (Explore Hanover)', 'Architectural landmarks include the historic Baker-Berry Library and the Hanover Inn (Norwich Inn)']
attractions.tips: Metered parking is enforced Monday through Saturday from 9 AM to 5 PM, but is free on Sundays and public holidays. The Hanover Parking Garage at 7 Lebanon Street offers all-day parking and is free on weekends. For a classic experience, visit Lou's Restaurant & Bakery, a local staple since 1947 (Town of Hanover, Visiting New England).
attractions.nearby: Dartmouth College, The Green, Hood Museum of Art, Hopkins Center for the Arts, Ledyard Canoe Club, Mink Brook Nature Preserve (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Hanover Inn Dartmouth
attractions.description: The Hanover Inn Dartmouth is a historic AAA Four-Diamond boutique hotel located on the campus of Dartmouth College. With a legacy spanning over two centuries, it offers 129 elegantly-appointed guest rooms and suites that blend New England charm with modern luxury. The property features sophisticated dining at PINE Restaurant and overlooks the iconic Dartmouth College Green. (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)
attractions.address: Two East Wheelock Street, Hanover, New Hampshire 03755
attractions.hours: Hotel: Open 24/7. PINE Restaurant: Mon-Fri Breakfast 7:00 AM–11:00 AM, Lunch 11:30 AM–2:00 PM, Dinner 5:00 PM–10:00 PM; Sat-Sun Breakfast 7:00 AM–11:00 AM, Brunch 11:30 AM–2:00 PM, Dinner 5:00 PM–10:00 PM. In-room Dining: Daily 7:00 AM–10:00 PM. (PINE Restaurant)
attractions.admission: Free (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)
highlights: 'AAA Four-Diamond boutique hotel rating ([Hanover Inn Dartmouth)', 'Prime location directly overlooking the Dartmouth College Green (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)', 'Home to the award-winning PINE Restaurant (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)', 'Member of the Historic Hotels of America (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)', 'Over 11,000 square feet of sophisticated meeting and event space (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)', 'Eco-friendly hotel initiatives including a partnership with Clean the World (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)']
attractions.tips: Valet parking is available for $32 per night. The property is dog-friendly for an additional fee, but reservations for pets must be made in advance. As a 100% smoke-free property, guests can enjoy a clean and fresh environment throughout their stay. (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)
attractions.nearby: Hopkins Center for the Arts, Hood Museum of Art, Baker-Berry Library, Nugget Theaters, Appalachian Trail (Hanover Inn Dartmouth)

attractions.name: Hood Museum of Art
attractions.description: The Hood Museum of Art at Dartmouth College is home to one of the oldest and largest college or university art collections in the United States, dating back to 1772. It features a diverse range of holdings, including significant American, Native American, European, African, and Melanesian art, as well as a major archive of photojournalism and ancient Mediterranean artifacts. The museum serves as a vital educational resource for the Dartmouth community and the Upper Valley region, offering frequently rotating exhibitions from its collection of over 70,000 objects (Hood Museum).
attractions.hours: Wednesday: 11:00 am – 5:00 pm; Thursday: 11:00 am – 5:00 pm; Friday: 11:00 am – 5:00 pm; Saturday: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm; Sunday: Closed; Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed (Hood Museum)
attractions.admission: Free and open to everyone (Hood Museum). Admission is free for all events as well (Hood Museum).
highlights: 'The Epic of American Civilization (1932–34) by José Clemente Orozco, a National Historic Landmark mural series ([Hood Museum)', 'Six 3,000-year-old Assyrian stone reliefs from the palace of Ashurnasirpal II (Hood Museum)', 'Extensive collection of indigenous Australian contemporary art (Hood Museum)', 'A major archive of photojournalism spanning various historical eras (Hood Museum)', "Masterpieces by artists such as Georgia O'Keeffe, Pablo Picasso, and Mark Rothko (Tripadvisor)", 'Renovated and expanded building designed by architects Tod Williams and Billie Tsien, featuring a welcoming atrium (Hood Museum)']
attractions.tips: One-word searches work best when exploring the online collection. Metered street parking is available nearby, and there is a paid garage at 7 Lebanon Street. The museum is fully accessible, and visitors can request assistive-listening devices for programs (Hood Museum). Don't miss the Orozco murals located in the nearby Baker Library basement, which are under the museum's supervision (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Dartmouth College campus, Hopkins Center for the Arts ("The Hop"), Baker-Berry Library, Montshire Museum of Science, and the Appalachian Trail which passes through downtown Hanover (Tripadvisor, Getaway Mavens).

attractions.name: Hopkins Center for the Arts at Dartmouth
attractions.description: The Hopkins Center for the Arts at Dartmouth is a multidisciplinary hub that cultivates art as central to life through diverse performances, films, and exhibitions. It serves as a catalyst for creative experiences, hosting over 100 live events and 200 film screenings annually in its newly renovated and expanded facilities. Hopkins Center for the Arts
attractions.address: 4 East Wheelock Street, Dartmouth College, Hanover, NH 03755
attractions.hours: Galleries: Tue–Sat 12:30–10 pm, Sun 12:30–5:30 pm; Box Office: Tue–Fri 12–5 pm, Sat 2–5 pm (also open one hour prior to all ticketed events); Closed Mondays. Venue Information, Visit

attractions.tips: Skips the lines by using mobile ticketing via your personal device. The house usually opens 30 minutes before showtime; avoid arriving earlier as entry is not guaranteed. On-street metered parking is available nearby (metered until 5 pm, Monday–Saturday). Visit & Policies

attractions.name: Ledyard Canoe Club
attractions.description: The Ledyard Canoe Club is a historic, 100-year-old outdoor organization and a sub-organization of the Dartmouth Outing Club. It operates a community rental business on the Connecticut River, offering canoes, kayaks, and paddleboards to the public during the warmer months. The club is well-known for producing Olympic athletes and organizing student-led expeditions to destinations worldwide (Ledyard Canoe Club, The Dartmouth).
attractions.hours: Weekdays: 12:00 PM – 6:00 PM, Weekends: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM (Ledyard Canoe Club)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the open season (spring through mid-October) (Frommer's, Ledyard Canoe Club)
attractions.admission: Canoe: $20/hr, Kayak: $10/hr, Paddle board: $15/hr, Clipper: $30/hr; Student rates are half price (Ledyard Canoe Club)
highlights: 'Fleet of canoes, kayaks, and stand-up paddleboards for rent ([Ledyard Canoe Club)', 'Access to the scenic Connecticut River and nearby Gilman Island (The Dartmouth)', 'Historic clubhouse serving as a hub for student and community paddling (Ledyard Canoe Club)', 'Annual events like Riverfest featuring whitewater races and slaloms (Upper Valley Business Alliance)', 'Year-long memberships available for regular paddlers (The Dartmouth)', 'Support for student-led international expeditions and local flatwater trips (Ledyard Canoe Club)']
attractions.tips: Free parking is available on weekends in the lot by Ledyard Bridge; renters can also obtain a complimentary parking pass for the club lot from the desk worker. The club typically opens when the water temperature reaches 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the spring and closes in the fall (Ledyard Canoe Club FAQ, The Dartmouth).
attractions.nearby: Dartmouth College Campus, Ledyard Bridge, Gilman Island, Titcomb Cabin, Connecticut River (The Dartmouth, Reddit)

attractions.name: McNutt Hall
attractions.description: McNutt Hall is a historic academic building at Dartmouth College that serves as the primary gateway for prospective students and visitors. Originally constructed in 1904 as the home for the Tuck School of Business, it now houses the Undergraduate Admissions Office, the Financial Aid Office, and the Office of the Registrar. The building is centrally located on the west side of the Green, making it a key landmark on the campus. Dartmouth Admissions, The Dartmouth
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed Dartmouth Admissions, Dartmouth Registrar
attractions.admission: Free (Includes visitor access and campus tours) Dartmouth Admissions
highlights: ['Primary hub for undergraduate admissions and information sessions.', 'Houses the Office of the Registrar and the Financial Aid Office.', 'Originally built in 1904 to house the Tuck School of Business.', 'Features historic architectural details, including a balcony railing with "TH" initials.', 'Strategically located on the west side of the historic Dartmouth Green.']
attractions.tips: Prospective students should register online in advance for official campus tours and information sessions which typically depart from or near McNutt Hall. While walk-in inquiries are welcome, visiting during mid-day is best as some offices close briefly for lunch between 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM. Dartmouth Admissions
attractions.nearby: The Dartmouth Green, Baker-Berry Library, Hood Museum of Art, Hopkins Center for the Arts, Parkhurst Hall

attractions.name: Occom Pond
attractions.description: Occom Pond is a scenic, man-made pond on the northern edge of the Dartmouth College campus, named after Reverend Samson Occom. It is a central hub for year-round recreation, famous for winter ice skating and summer 'woccoms' (walks around Occom). The pond is bordered by the Dartmouth Outing Club House, private homes, and the Hanover Country Club golf course.
attractions.address: Occom Ridge & Clement Road, Hanover, NH 03755
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM – Sunset (Standard skating hours); Extended to 9:00 PM when lights are installed in winter. General pond access is daily.
attractions.admission: Free (General access); Skate/equipment rentals at the DOC House typically free for Dartmouth students and available for a fee to the public.
highlights: ['Winter ice skating and pond hockey on the frozen surface', "The 'Woccom' – a popular 1-mile walking loop around the pond", 'Dartmouth Outing Club (DOC) House located on the northern shore', "Proximity to Pine Park's extensive hiking and cross-country ski trails", 'Host to Dartmouth Winter Carnival activities and Winter Fun Days', 'Scenic views of the surrounding pine-shaded banks and Occom Ridge']
attractions.tips: Visit during winter for the full experience of outdoor ice skating and hot chocolate at the DOC House. In other seasons, enjoy a sunset walk (a 'woccom') along the well-maintained loop. Check 'Dartmouth Outdoors' for current ice thickness and skating conditions.
attractions.nearby: Pine Park, Hanover Country Club, Dartmouth College Green, Ledyard Canoe Club, Hood Museum of Art, Connecticut River

attractions.name: Quechee Gorge (Quechee State Park) Vermont State Parks
attractions.description: Known as "Vermont's Little Grand Canyon," Quechee Gorge is the deepest gorge in the state, reaching a depth of 165 feet. Carved by glacial activity over 13,000 years ago, it is the centerpiece of Quechee State Park and offers breathtaking views of the Ottauquechee River. Vermont State Parks, Wikipedia

attractions.hours: Daily 10:00 AM - Official Sunset (Park); 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Visitor Center) Vermont State Parks, Hartford Area Chamber of Commerce

attractions.admission: Adults (14+): $5.00; Children (4-13): $2.00; Small Child (0-3): Free Vermont State Parks Fees
highlights: 'Viewing the 165-foot deep gorge from the historic Quechee Gorge Bridge [Wikipedia', 'Hiking the easy, accessible trails along the mile-long chasm Vermont State Parks', 'Whitewater kayaking or swimming in the Ottauquechee River Wikipedia', 'Camping at the Quechee State Park campground with cabins and tent sites Vermont State Parks', 'Visiting the Quechee Gorge Visitor Center for local history and area maps Hartford Area Chamber of Commerce']
attractions.tips: The bridge offers the best panoramic views without a hike. Pack a picnic to enjoy at the state park's designated areas. If visiting in fall, arrive early as foliage season is extremely popular. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Rauner Special Collections Library
attractions.description: Housed in the historic Webster Hall, Rauner Special Collections Library is the primary repository for Dartmouth College's rare books, manuscripts, and archives. The library features a striking modern design with a four-story glass-enclosed stacks system situated within the building's original 1907 auditorium architecture. It serves as both a high-level research facility and a welcoming space for the public to explore unique historical artifacts. (Wikipedia, Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.hours: Sunday: Closed; Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:45 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:45 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:45 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:45 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:45 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM (Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.admission: Free (Dartmouth Libraries)

attractions.tips: Visitors must check in at the main desk and present a photo ID to view materials. Large bags, coats, food, and pens must be stored in provided lockers; only pencils, paper, and laptops/phones are permitted in the reading room. (Dartmouth Libraries)

attractions.name: Red Rolfe Field at Biondi Park
attractions.description: Red Rolfe Field at Biondi Park is the home of the Dartmouth Big Green baseball team and is recognized as one of the top small venues in Division I baseball. The facility features a synthetic FieldTurf surface, a modern press box, and permanent seating for 650 spectators, with a total capacity of approximately 2,000. It was extensively renovated in 2008 following a $5.2 million gift from alumni Michael and Cynthia Biondi. Dartmouth College Athletics, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Rollins Chapel
attractions.description: Rollins Chapel is a historic, non-denominational sacred space located on the campus of Dartmouth College. Built in 1885, it serves as an interfaith center for worship, prayer, and meditation, featuring a unique Richardsonian Romanesque architectural style and a community prayer labyrinth. (Dartmouth College)

attractions.name: Rupert C. Thompson Arena (Thompson Arena)
attractions.description: Rupert C. Thompson Arena is a 3,500-seat ice hockey facility located on the campus of Dartmouth College in Hanover, New Hampshire. It serves as the home ice for the Dartmouth Big Green men's and women's varsity hockey teams and is renowned for its barrel-vaulted reinforced concrete architecture designed by Pier Luigi Nervi. The arena also hosts public skating sessions and community events throughout the winter season. Dartmouth Athletics, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Sanborn Library (Sanborn House)
attractions.description: Sanborn Library is a historic, Georgian-style study hall known for its 'dark academia' aesthetic and rich traditions. As the home of Dartmouth's Department of English and Creative Writing, it features hand-carved butternut paneling, a marble fireplace, and ornate chandeliers. It is most famous for its long-standing daily tea ritual, which fosters a sense of community and academic collegiality. (Dartmouth News, The Dartmouth)
attractions.address: 19 North Main Street, Sanborn House, Room 201
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Fri: 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sat: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sun: 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM (English and Creative Writing)

attractions.tips: Visit during the academic term at 4:00 PM to experience the historic tea tradition. Please maintain silence as it is a dedicated study space outside of tea hour. (The Dartmouth)

attractions.name: Spaulding Auditorium
attractions.description: Spaulding Auditorium is a 792-seat concert hall and film theater renowned for its exceptional acoustics and distinctive teak-walled trapezoid stage. As the primary performance venue for the Hopkins Center for the Arts at Dartmouth College, it hosts a diverse range of international artists, student ensembles, and film screenings. The facility, designed by architect Wallace Harrison, serves as a vital cultural hub for both the university and the greater Upper Valley community (Dartmouth Hopkins Center, Wikipedia).

attractions.name: The Epic of American Civilization (Orozco Murals)
attractions.description: The Epic of American Civilization is a monumental fresco mural cycle created by Mexican artist José Clemente Orozco between 1932 and 1934. Spanning 24 panels and approximately 3,200 square feet, the work explores the history of the North American continent from the perspective of indigenous and European cultural currents, depicting themes of migration, sacrifice, and the impact of modern industrialization. It is widely considered one of the most significant works of Mexican muralism in the United States and was designated a National Historic Landmark in 2013.
attractions.address: 25 North Main Street, Hanover, NH 03755 (Lower Level of Baker-Berry Library)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 am – 12:00 am, Tuesday: 8:00 am – 12:00 am, Wednesday: 8:00 am – 12:00 am, Thursday: 8:00 am – 12:00 am, Friday: 8:00 am – 12:00 am, Saturday: 10:00 am – 12:00 am, Sunday: 10:00 am – 12:00 am (Dartmouth Libraries)
attractions.admission: Free (Hood Museum of Art)
attractions.phone: 603-646-2808 (Hood Museum of Art), 603-646-2567 (Baker-Berry Library) (Hood Museum of Art, Dartmouth Libraries)
highlights: 'Designated as a National Historic Landmark in 2013 ([Hood Museum of Art)', 'A cycle of 24 fresco panels covering nearly 3,200 square feet of wall space (National Park Service)', 'Depicts the myth of Quetzalcoatl and the Spanish Conquest by Hernán Cortés (Wikipedia)', "Critically examines modern industrial society and the 'Gods of the Modern World' (National Park Service)", 'Integrated into a functional library reading room, making it a unique public art space (Dartmouth Admissions)']
attractions.tips: Flash photography is strictly prohibited; however, photography for personal use is allowed (Hood Museum of Art). Visitors can access a self-guided audio tour via the Hood Museum's website or the Dartmouth Digital Orozco app (National Park Service). Since the room is a quiet study area for students, visitors are encouraged to be respectful of the silent atmosphere (The Dartmouth).

attractions.name: The Green (formally the College Green)
attractions.description: The Green is a five-acre grass-covered field and common space that serves as the historic and emotional heart of Dartmouth College. Originally cleared in 1770, it has evolved from a cattle pasture and athletic field into a central hub for major campus traditions, student rallies, and daily recreation.
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Homecoming Bonfire: A massive bonfire constructed annually by the first-year class each fall.', 'Winter Carnival Snow Sculpture: The site of an elaborate, multi-story snow sculpture built every February.', 'Green Key Weekend: A springtime tradition featuring outdoor music festivals and community performances.', "Gravel Walking Paths: Seven historic gravel paths that have defined the Green's layout since 1931.", "Commencement Ceremonies: The traditional setting for Dartmouth's graduation, held outdoors regardless of weather.", 'Wi-Fi Connectivity: The entire common area is fully Wi-Fi-enabled for students and visitors.']
attractions.tips: Visitors can enjoy a picnic on the grass or join informal games of frisbee and spikeball. You can borrow sports equipment for free at the nearby Collis Center for Student Involvement. For the best experience, visit during a guided campus tour or during one of the major seasonal festivals.

attractions.name: Wilson Hall
attractions.category: University Building / Historic Landmark (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine)
attractions.description: Wilson Hall is a historic Romanesque building at Dartmouth College, originally constructed in 1885 as the college's first dedicated library building (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine). Designed by Samuel J. F. Thayer, it features dark red brick and sandstone trim, reflecting the influence of H. H. Richardson's architectural style (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine). Today, it serves as an administrative and academic hub, housing the Hopkins Center for the Arts offices and the Department of Middle Eastern Studies (Hopkins Center).
attractions.address: 8 East Wheelock Street, Hanover, NH 03755 (Dartmouth Campus Map)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Standard Office Hours); Student/Faculty access may vary (Yelp, Office of Student Life)
attractions.admission: Free (Dartmouth Admissions)
highlights: "Originally served as Dartmouth College's first dedicated library building upon its completion in 1885 ([Samuel J. F. Thayer).", 'Architectural design by Samuel J. F. Thayer in the Romanesque Revival style with distinctive red sandstone trim (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine).', 'Currently houses the Department of Middle Eastern Studies and administrative offices for the Hopkins Center for the Arts (Hopkins Center).', 'Centrally located on the southern edge of the Dartmouth Green, adjacent to the Hood Museum of Art (Theater Department).', 'Historically served as the College museum for decades before being repurposed for academic and arts administration (Dartmouth Alumni Magazine).']
attractions.tips: Visitors should start their tour at the nearby Dartmouth Green and check in at the Admissions Office if visiting as a prospective student (Dartmouth Admissions). Comfortable walking shoes are recommended as most campus highlights are best explored on foot (Dartmouth Admissions).

Founded by U.S. Marine veteran Lou Bressett, the quaint diner-style establishment is famous for its all-day breakfast, full-service bakery, and deep-rooted connection to the Dartmouth College community. It offers a warm, welcoming atmosphere featuring 'from-scratch' modern diner cuisine, ranging from classic American favorites to authentic Mexican-inspired dishes.

attractions.name: Mink Brook Nature Preserve
attractions.description: The Mink Brook Nature Preserve is a 112-acre sanctuary that protects vital habitats for wild brook trout, black bears, and a diverse array of flora and fauna. The preserve features scenic trails winding through upland forests of white pine and hemlock along the boulder-filled Mink Brook. It serves as a peaceful natural retreat just south of downtown Hanover, popular for birdwatching and wildflower viewing (Hanover Conservancy).
attractions.hours: Open daily, year-round; daylight hours recommended (MyHikes, Hanover Conservancy).
attractions.admission: Free (MyHikes), consistent with Hanover Conservancy policies.
highlights: 'Quinn Trail: A stroller and wheelchair-accessible path that connects to the Tanzi Tract ([Hanover Conservancy).', 'Wildlife Habitat: Home to black bears, wild brook trout, and over 70 recorded bird species (Hanover Conservancy, Trail Finder).', 'Botanical Diversity: Features over 130 species of native herbaceous plants and 20 species of ferns (Hanover Conservancy).', 'Mink Brook Crossing: A dramatic log suspension bridge crossing the dynamic watercourse (Hanover Conservancy).', 'Norman Overlook: A scenic viewpoint along the Quinn Trail offering quiet enjoyment of the brook (Hanover Conservancy).', 'Mink Brook Quest: An educational treasure hunt created for visitors to explore the preserve (Hanover Conservancy).']
attractions.tips: Bicycles are prohibited to protect wildlife habitat and water quality (Hanover Conservancy). Pets must be under direct control, and owners are required to pick up and remove pet waste (Hanover Conservancy). Visitors should wear comfortable walking shoes as some trails can be uneven (Evendo). No restrooms or trash bins are available at the site (MyHikes).
attractions.nearby: Angelo Tanzi Natural Area, Connecticut River, Dartmouth College, Mink Brook West, and Indian Ridge trails in Lebanon (Hanover Conservancy).

It is located in the historic 1882 Whipple Building and is known for its cozy atmosphere and commitment to hosting live entertainment without a cover charge.

attractions.name: Appalachian National Scenic Trail (Hanover Section)
attractions.description: The Hanover section of the Appalachian Trail is a unique and historic segment where the trail runs directly down the town's Main Street. Maintained by the Dartmouth Outing Club since 1929, this portion connects the rugged wilderness of the White Mountains with the academic charm of Dartmouth College. It is one of only ten towns where the trail and main street coincide, offering hikers a rare opportunity to walk through a bustling Ivy League community. Appalachian Trail Conservancy, Dartmouth Libraries

attractions.name: Connecticut River (Hanover Shoreline)
attractions.description: The Connecticut River is the most significant natural and recreational resource in Hanover, serving as a designated American Heritage River with excellent water quality. It offers a scenic corridor for walking, bird watching, and paddling, with major access points at Wilson's Landing and the Ledyard Canoe Club. The riverfront also features rare floodplain forests and is a hub for regional crew races and outdoor education.

attractions.tips: Visit Wilson's Landing at sunset for exceptional views, or rent a kayak from Ledyard Canoe Club to explore Gilman Island. Note that a New Hampshire fishing license is required for angling on the river.

Known for its rigorous undergraduate liberal arts curriculum, prestigious professional schools, and deep-rooted campus traditions, it is situated on a scenic 269-acre campus in the Upper Valley region. The college emphasizes a close-knit community and a unique year-round academic calendar known as the D-Plan. Wikipedia, Dartmouth Official

It preserves and interprets the historic core of the original Enfield Shaker Village, featuring nine Shaker-built structures, gardens, and the iconic Great Stone Dwelling. Enfield Shaker Museum

attractions.name: Gile Mountain
attractions.description: Gile Mountain is a prominent peak in the 290-acre Woody Adams Conservation Forest, known for its historic fire tower and exceptional views. A short 0.7-mile hike leads visitors to a 70-foot summit tower that offers a 360-degree panorama of the Upper Valley, encompassing the Green Mountains and White Mountains. The area serves as a popular destination for hikers, mountain bikers, and nature enthusiasts seeking long-distance vistas Norwich Trails, Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Hanover Area Farmers Market on the Green
attractions.description: The Hanover Area Farmers Market is a vibrant, agriculturally-focused community gathering spot held on the iconic Dartmouth College Green. It features a wide array of fresh local produce, meats, herbs, cheeses, and flowers, alongside locally prepared foods and handcrafted artisanal goods. The market serves as a central hub for residents and visitors to enjoy live local music, artful demonstrations, and sustainability education while supporting regional farmers.

attractions.hours: Wednesday: 3:00 PM – 6:00 PM (June through mid-October)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Fresh locally-sourced produce, meats, and artisan cheeses', 'Live performances by local musicians every week', 'Handcrafted jewelry, crafts, and unique household products', 'Selection of home-baked goods and prepared gourmet foods', 'Annual special events such as heirloom tomato tastings', 'Direct interaction with local farmers and food artisans']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to bring reusable bags or coolers for fresh items and to carry cash, as some vendors may not accept credit cards. Arrive early for the best selection of popular items like specialty cheeses and fresh-cut flowers. Parking is available nearby on Wheelock Street and College Street.

attractions.name: Kendal Riverfront Park
attractions.description: Kendal Riverfront Park is a 10-acre waterfront green space located along the Connecticut River that provides public access to prime riverfront property formerly occupied by the Chieftain Motel. The park serves as a key access point for rowing and community recreation, featuring a loop trail that connects to the Rinker-Steele Natural Area and offers scenic views of New England's largest river. (Hanover Conservancy, Kendal at Hanover)
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Hanover Conservancy)
attractions.admission: Free (Hanover Conservancy)

attractions.tips: General public vehicle access to the waterfront is prohibited; visitors should use the gravel parking lot and walk down the short, steep drive. Trails can be slippery when wet due to clay soils, and leashed dogs are welcome. (Hanover Conservancy, Town of Hanover)

attractions.name: Lyme Common Historic District (Lyme Village)
attractions.description: The Lyme Common Historic District encompasses the original historic center of Lyme, New Hampshire, established in 1781. It features over sixty historic buildings surrounding a long, narrow town common, representing a well-preserved 19th-century village center with notable Federal and Greek Revival architecture. (Wikipedia, NH Magazine)

attractions.name: Mascoma Lake
attractions.description: Mascoma Lake is a 1,158-acre lake located in western New Hampshire, primarily within the town of Enfield and partially in Lebanon. The lake is fed by the Mascoma and Knox Rivers and is a popular destination for year-round recreation, including trout fishing, boating, and swimming. It is known for its scenic beauty and its historical connection to the Enfield Shaker community. (Wikipedia, Mascoma Lake Association)
attractions.hours: Dawn to Dusk (Public Access Areas) (Lebanon NH)
attractions.admission: Free (Shaker Museum)
highlights: 'Recreational fishing for rainbow trout and other species ([Wikipedia)', 'Paddling and boating with public ramp access (The Paddling Hub)', 'Swimming at the public Shakoma Town Beach (Enfield NH)', 'Winter sports including ice fishing and skating trails (Mascoma Lake Skating Association)', 'Wildlife observation of loons and bald eagles (Mascoma Lake Association)', 'Proximity to the historic Enfield Shaker Museum (Shaker Museum)']
attractions.tips: Morning hours offer the calmest water for paddling; check NHDES for cyanobacteria alerts before swimming; polarized sunglasses are recommended to see submerged obstacles. (The Paddling Hub, Mascoma Lake Association)

attractions.name: Nathan's Garden
attractions.description: Nathan's Garden is a one-acre botanical sanctuary in Hanover, NH, created in 1991 by the Hall family to honor the memory of Nathan Hall. This beloved neighborhood green space features a diverse collection of over forty wildflower species and native plants, providing a peaceful environment for reflection. Visitors can explore the grounds via a variety of winding paths and enjoy quiet moments on the provided benches. (Hanover Conservancy, TripAdvisor)

attractions.admission: Free (Hanover Conservancy)

attractions.name: Norwich Farmers' Market
attractions.description: The Norwich Farmers' Market is one of the oldest and largest farmers' markets in northern New England, featuring over 50 local vendors from the Upper Valley. Since 1977, it has provided a vibrant community hub for fresh seasonal produce, artisanal cheeses, local meats, and high-quality handmade crafts. The market operates outdoors in a scenic meadow during summer and moves to the historic Tracy Hall for the winter season. (Official Website), (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park
attractions.description: Saint-Gaudens National Historical Park preserves the home, studios, and gardens of Augustus Saint-Gaudens, one of America’s greatest sculptors. The 190-acre site features over 100 of his works, ranging from delicate cameos to heroic-size bronze monuments, all set against a beautiful mountain backdrop. NPS Home, National Park Planner
attractions.hours: Grounds and trails: Open year-round during daylight hours. Visitor Center and Park Buildings (May 24 – October 31): Thursday through Monday, 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM. Aspet (Historic House): Open select hours (typically 10:00 AM – 11:45 PM and 2:00 PM – 3:45 PM) as staffing allows. NPS Hours, National Park Planner
attractions.days_open: Buildings: Thursday through Monday (closed Tuesday/Wednesday); Grounds: Daily (Year-round). NPS Hours
attractions.admission: Standard Entrance Pass: $10.00 (adults 16+), children 15 and younger are free; Annual Entrance Park Pass: $40.00. National Park Passes (e.g., America the Beautiful) are accepted. Cashless site, payment by credit/debit card only. NPS Fees, Park Ranger John
highlights: ['Aspet, the historic home of Augustus Saint-Gaudens, featuring original family furniture and possessions.', 'The Little Studio, where the sculptor worked, and the New Gallery displaying a collection of his original works.', 'Heroic-size bronze casts of famous sculptures including the Standing Lincoln and the Shaw Memorial.', 'Extensive landscaped gardens, terraced lawns, and three miles of scenic hiking trails like the Ravine Trail.', 'Blow-Me-Down Farm, part of the historic Cornish Colony of artists.']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the site is spread across 190 acres with some hilly terrain. Check the tour schedule at the Visitor Center upon arrival, as house tours have limited capacity and specific time slots. Note that the park is cashless and only accepts credit or debit cards for fees. Tripadvisor Reviews, Yelp

attractions.description: Housed in a restored 19th-century woolen mill, this flagship location features a turbine-powered glassblowing studio fueled by the Ottauquechee River. Visitors can watch master artisans craft lead-free crystal glassware before dining at the award-winning restaurant overlooking a picturesque waterfall and covered bridge.
attractions.hours: Store Hours: Mon-Tue 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Wed-Thu 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Glassblowing Hours: Mon-Tue 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Wed-Sun 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Restaurant: Lunch Daily 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, Dinner Wed-Thu & Sun 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM, Fri-Sat 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM

As part of the Diocese of New Hampshire, the church embraces diversity and invites people of all backgrounds to join in its joyful worship, timeless traditions, and thriving campus ministry. The parish is known for its Gothic architecture, dynamic music program, and commitment to serving the community through radical hospitality.

attractions.name: Town of Woodstock, Vermont
attractions.description: Woodstock is a quintessential New England village often described as the "prettiest small town in America." It features a historic downtown with beautifully preserved Federal-style homes, locally-owned boutiques, and an iconic village green, all nestled along the Ottauquechee River. The town serves as a year-round destination known for its rich history of conservation, outdoor recreation, and vibrant seasonal festivals like Wassail Weekend.
